rent.note: furnished 1BR in city centre; $350–$550 range; District 1 up to €900

utilities.note: electric + heating + water + internet; ~£151 avg for 85m2

diningOut.note: Floreasca and Dorobanți cafes; good value

transit.note: metro + bus monthly pass ~$20; excellent metro

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; Bucharest strong tech/startup scene

simData.note: Romanian SIM; Digi/Orange; cheapest mobile in EU

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. CEE capital; mild seasonality.

evidence.claim: 1BR city centre $350–$550; nomad can live well on €740–€1,100/mo
evidence.quote: most solo nomads find they can live quite well on a monthly budget between €740 and €1,100, covering a private apartment, utilities, groceries, and plenty of social outings

evidence.claim: 1BR centre avg £489 ($615); rent $550 city centre in nomad estimate
evidence.quote: Rent in Bucharest ranges from $350/month (outside city center) to $550/month (city center) for a one-bedroom apartment

evidence.claim: utilities avg £151/mo for 85m2; transit pass $20; groceries $170
evidence.quote: rent ($550 for a 1BR in city center), groceries ($170), utilities ($120), and a monthly transit pass ($20)

evidence.claim: Bucharest hidden gem for nomads: super-fast internet, Romania DNV valid 12 months
evidence.quote: Bucharest is a hidden gem for digital nomads with super-fast internet and very low costs, and Romania offers a digital nomad visa valid for 12 months
